experiments.description: Baseline SAC+HER, unmodified. 256x3 MLP, batch 256, buffer 200k, HER k=4 future, STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING=1000, UPDATE_EVERY=1, N_UPDATES=1.
experiments.hypothesis: Baseline establishes the reference point. Expect low success_rate because the 10s budget combined with STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING=1000 leaves very few gradient updates available for SAC to actually learn anything.
experiments.vlm_feedback_summary: Robot ignores the goal entirely; arm collapses downward into the table across all 3 episodes. In ep1 the gripper actually starts within the success threshold of the goal but the deterministic policy actively walks AWAY from it. Policy is effectively untrained.
vlm_failure_modes: walks away from goal (no goal-tracking behavior)
arm collapses into table (strong downward bias in policy output)
ignores desired_goal portion of obs (similar end pose across different goals)
fails to exploit favorable starts (ep1 starts at goal, policy still leaves)
vlm_suggestions: STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING 1000 -> 100 (single highest-leverage change, doubles available gradient updates)
N_UPDATES 1 -> 4 (REDQ-style UTD, multiplies effective gradient signal)
dense reward shaping in training (-distance instead of sparse -1/0) for stronger gradient signal
smaller network (128x2 or 64x2) since obs is only 13-dim
experiments.lesson_learned: On a 10s wall-clock budget, the STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING=1000 random warmup consumes ~54% of total env steps before any gradient update fires. Only 853 SAC updates total, far too few to train a 410k-param net even on FetchReach. The dominant constraint here is gradient steps, not env experience.

experiments.description: STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING 1000 -> 100. Single-variable change to test whether earlier learning lifts gradient-update count within 10s budget.
experiments.hypothesis: Lowering the random pre-learning warmup from 1000 to 100 will roughly double total_updates within the same 10s budget (expecting ~1700 from ~1850 total steps), enough to lift success_rate above 0.0.
experiments.vlm_feedback_summary: Robot still ignores goals; no goal-tracking behavior visible. ep2 shows a new failure mode (retract arm upward) not seen in exp1, but ep0/ep1 still collapse into the table. mean_distance dropped 0.61 -> 0.40 but driven by random end-pose distribution, not by reaching toward goals.
vlm_failure_modes: new failure: arm retracts upward into vertical posture (ep2)
policy direction is inconsistent across episodes — different default poses for similar starts
vlm_suggestions: STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING change is neutral for total_updates; don't pursue alone
next attack: dense reward shaping in replay buffer (-distance instead of sparse)
if dense reward unlocks >0% success, layer UTD>1 (N_UPDATES=2-4) on exp 4
experiments.lesson_learned: GPU throughput per gradient update is the binding constraint, not env-step count. Random pre-learning rollouts are essentially free (no sample, no grad), so reducing them barely affects total_updates (853 -> 890, +4.3%). Total env steps DROP (1853 -> 990) because once learning starts, every step pays for action sampling + grad update, slowing env-step rate to ~90 steps/sec. To get more updates in 10s, need faster grad steps (smaller net/batch) OR better sample efficiency per step (dense reward, n-step returns, REDQ/DroQ).

insights: Gradient-step budget on a 10s wall-clock is ~850-900 updates regardless of STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING. The binding constraint is GPU throughput per grad step (~85-90 upd/s), NOT env-step count. Random pre-learning steps are free; learning steps cost ~10ms each.
Total_steps is misleading on this setup — exp1 had 1853 total steps but only 853 grad updates because the first 1000 were free random rollouts. Don't optimize total_steps; optimize total_updates.
failed_directions: STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING 1000 -> 100 alone (exp2): only +4% updates, 0% success_rate. The lever was wrong; not the warmup but the per-update cost.
promising_directions: Dense reward shaping in replay buffer (-distance instead of sparse {-1, 0}) — biggest expected lift per literature for goal-conditioned tasks
Smaller/faster network (e.g. 128x2) — more grad steps per second within the same 10s budget
Raise UTD (N_UPDATES) — more grad signal per env step, may need DroQ-style dropout/LayerNorm if Q-values diverge
n-step returns (n=3-5) on the critic for stronger value targets given dense reward
